message:
feat(net): support bind/reuseaddr/reuseport configuration before TcpSocket::connect

Remove the Phase 0 restriction that rejected connect() when bind, reuseaddr,
or reuseport had been configured on the TcpSocket. Instead, construct a
socket2::Socket that applies set_reuse_address, set_reuse_port (unix-only),
and bind() before connecting, enabling use cases like source-address pinning
and port reuse for high-connection-count scenarios.

Implementation:
- socket.rs: Replace the simple TcpStream::connect(addr) with full socket2::Socket
  construction that respects all pre-configured options before connecting
- stream.rs: Extract post-socket-creation logic into pub(crate) connect_from_socket()
  helper so TcpSocket can pass in a pre-configured socket

Test: Replace test_connect_rejects_configuration (which validated the old
restriction) with test_connect_with_bind_success performing a real
bind+connect round-trip against a local listener.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
